meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your freelance workflow.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing why your code breaks. By implementing event-based analytics, you turn opaque runtime errors into actionable data streams. This approach identifies the exact state, user path, and environmental variables leading to failure, allowing you to fix bugs in minutes rather than hours of tedious manual reproduction.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, the client is pinging you on Slack, and your code is throwing an error that refuses to replicate. This is the freelancer's purgatory: the 'ghost bug' that drains your billable hours and tests your patience. You aren't just missing a semicolon; you are missing visibility into the live execution environment.

Modern engineering isn't about writing perfect code; it's about building systems that tell you exactly where they fail. By integrating event-driven analytics, you transform your application into a self-reporting asset. This guide provides the blueprint to turn your debugging process from a guessing game into a surgical operation.

steps.title: Define Critical Error Triggers
steps.description: Identify the high-risk functions in your codebase where silent failures occur. We need to define event schemas that capture the payload and current state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase and suggest 5 critical checkpoints for event logging that would capture state before a runtime crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify high-risk failure points in ./src and suggest event tracking middleware'

steps.title: Inject Telemetry Middleware
steps.description: Wrap your core functions with telemetry wrappers to ensure every execution path is tracked without bloating your primary logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable telemetry middleware pattern that captures function arguments, return values, and timestamps for error reporting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'create telemetry wrapper for all functions in /services'

steps.title: Configure Error Contextualization
steps.description: Standard logs are useless without context. Attach user ID, browser session, and local state to every error event sent to your analytics provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error interceptor that automatically attaches global context and user session metadata to any caught exception.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'implement global error boundary with context injection'

steps.title: Deploy Real-Time Alerting
steps.description: Connect your analytics pipeline to a notification channel so you see the error before the client does.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a configuration script for a webhook that triggers a notification when error frequency exceeds a threshold of 3 per minute.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure webhook alerting for anomaly detection in telemetry stream'

steps.title: Validate Debugging Loop
steps.description: Simulate a failure to verify that the analytics dashboard captures the event accurately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that forces a controlled error and verifies the telemetry event is dispatched correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'run verification suite for error logging telemetry'

pro_tips: Log the 'last known good state' before an error occurs to enable instant replay of the failure condition.
Use sampling for high-traffic apps to keep your analytics costs low while maintaining 100% error-event capture.
Tag your logs with the git commit hash to instantly correlate new bugs with specific deployments.
Implement 'breadcrumb' logs that record the last 10 user interactions before a crash.
Always sanitize sensitive user data (PII) at the middleware layer before sending events to external analytics services.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, set up a dashboard in your analytics tool of choice to visualize error spikes and start mapping them to your deployment history.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on blind debugging and start shipping with the confidence of a lead engineer. Our community is built for those who refuse to settle for slow, buggy development cycles. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
